title: WiFi Connection Problems
description: Troubleshooting wireless network connectivity issues including connected but no internet.

steps.title: Toggle WiFi Off and On
steps.instruction:
*🔄 Toggle WiFi Off and On*
• Turn WiFi OFF from your taskbar/settings, wait 10 seconds, then turn it back ON.
• Try reconnecting to your network and check if internet works.
_Expected: WiFi reconnects and internet access is restored._

steps.title: Forget and Reconnect to Network
steps.instruction:
*📶 Forget and Reconnect to Network*
• Open WiFi settings → Click on your network name → Select *Forget Network*.
• Search for the network again and reconnect by entering the WiFi password.
_Expected: Fresh connection resolves authentication or config issues._

steps.title: Restart the WiFi Router
steps.instruction:
*🔌 Restart the WiFi Router*
• Unplug the WiFi router from the power socket.
• Wait 30 seconds, then plug it back in.
• Wait 2 minutes for it to fully restart before reconnecting.
_Expected: Router restarts and provides fresh internet connection._

steps.title: Run Windows Network Troubleshooter
steps.instruction:
*🛠️ Run Windows Network Troubleshooter*
• Go to *Settings → System → Troubleshoot → Other Troubleshooters*.
• Click *Run* next to *Internet Connections*.
• Follow the on-screen steps to detect and fix issues automatically.
_Expected: Windows identifies and fixes the network problem._

steps.title: Check Network Adapter
steps.instruction:
*🖥️ Check Network Adapter*
• Press *Windows + X* → Open *Device Manager*.
• Expand *Network Adapters* → Right-click your WiFi adapter → Select *Disable Device*, wait 5 seconds, then *Enable Device*.
• If you see a yellow warning icon, select *Update Driver*.
_Expected: Adapter resets and reconnects to WiFi._
